operators.name: About
operators.description: Return information about an expression

operators.name: Abs
operators.description: Absolute value (magnitude) of a number.

operators.name: AbsArg
operators.description: Tuple of magnitude and argument of a complex number.

operators.name: Add
operators.description: Sum of two or more values.

operators.name: AdjugateMatrix
operators.description: Adjugate (classical adjoint) of a square matrix.

operators.name: AGM
operators.description: Arithmetic-geometric mean. AGM(z) is shorthand for AGM(1, z) (Fungrim convention).

operators.name: AiryAi
operators.description: Airy function of the first kind

operators.name: AiryBi
operators.description: Airy function of the second kind

operators.name: Annotated
operators.description: Attach metadata or style annotations to an expression.

operators.name: Apart
operators.description: Alias for PartialFraction. Decompose a rational expression into partial fractions.

operators.name: AppellF1
operators.signature: (number, number, number, number, number, number) -> number
operators.description: Appell hypergeometric function F₁(a; b₁, b₂; c; x, y), double series for |x|, |y| < 1.

operators.name: Apply
operators.description: Apply a function to a list of arguments

operators.name: Arctan
operators.description: Inverse tangent.

operators.name: Arctan2
operators.description: Two-argument arctangent giving the angle of a vector.

operators.name: Argument
operators.description: Complex argument (phase angle) of a number.

operators.name: AsHsl
operators.description: Convert any color to HSL (hue degrees, s/l 0-1)

operators.name: AsHsv
operators.description: Convert any color to HSV (hue degrees, s/v 0-1)

operators.name: AsOklab
operators.description: Convert any color to OKLab

operators.name: AsOklch
operators.description: Convert any color to OKLCh

operators.name: AsRgb
operators.description: Convert any color to sRGB (channels 0-1)

operators.name: Assign
operators.description: Assign a value to a symbol or define a sequence. The RHS is evaluated immediately and `ce.assign(name, val)` mutates the binding in the current scope chain. When used inside a `Block`, the assignment is visible to subsequent statements in the block (sequential semantics).

operators.name: Assume
operators.description: Assume a type for a symbol

operators.name: At
operators.description:
Access an element of an indexed collection.

If the index is negative, it is counted from the end.

Multiple indices can be provided to access nested collections (e.g., matrices).

If the index is a finite collection of booleans, returns the elements where the mask is True.

If the index is a finite collection of integers, returns the elements at those indices.

operators.name: BaseForm
operators.description: `BaseForm(expr, base=10)`

operators.name: BellNumber
operators.description: Compute the Bell number B(n), the number of partitions of a set of n elements.

operators.name: BernoulliB
operators.description: Return the nth Bernoulli number Bₙ as an exact rational, using the convention B₁ = -1/2. Odd `n > 1` give 0.

operators.name: BesselI
operators.description: Modified Bessel function of the first kind

operators.name: BesselJ
operators.description: Bessel function of the first kind

operators.name: BesselK
operators.description: Modified Bessel function of the second kind (Macdonald function)

operators.name: BesselY
operators.description: Bessel function of the second kind (Neumann function)

operators.name: Beta
operators.description: Euler beta function

operators.name: BinCounts
operators.description: Count the number of elements falling into each bin.

operators.name: Binomial
operators.description: Compute the binomial coefficient C(n, k) = n! / (k! (n-k)!). Agrees with Choose for all defined values.

operators.name: Block
operators.description:
Evaluate a sequence of expressions in a local scope, **sequentially**. Each operand is evaluated in order; later operands observe side effects (`Assign`, `Declare`) of earlier operands. The block's value is the value of the last expression. Short-circuiting heads (`Return`, `Break`, `Continue`) terminate the sequence early.

IMPORTANT — consumers translating *simultaneous* action tuples (e.g. Desmos `(a → 1, b → a + 1)` where `b` reads the *pre-action* `a`) must rewrite to a snapshot-then-commit Block: bind each RHS to a fresh temp first, then assign the temps to the LHS symbols. See `docs/architecture/actions-and-randomness.md` for the canonical recipe.

operators.name: Boole
operators.description: Return 1 if the argument is true, 0 otherwise. Also known as the Iverson bracket

operators.name: BuiltinFunction
operators.description: Return a built-in function symbol by name.

operators.name: Cancel
operators.description: Cancel common polynomial factors in the numerator and denominator of a rational expression. Example: Cancel((x² - 1)/(x - 1), x) → x + 1

operators.name: CanonicalForm
operators.description:
Return the canonical form of an expression

Can be used to sort arguments of an expression.

Sorting arguments of commutative functions is a weak form of canonicalization that can be useful in some cases, for example to accept "x+1" and "1+x" while rejecting "x+1" and "2x-x+1"

operators.name: CarmichaelLambda
operators.description: Return the Carmichael function λ(n) (the reduced totient): the smallest positive integer `m` such that `a^m ≡ 1 (mod n)` for every `a` coprime to `n`. Defined for `n ≥ 1`.

operators.name: CartesianProduct
operators.description: Return the Cartesian product of input sets.

operators.name: CatalanNumber
operators.description: Return the nth Catalan number `C(n) = (2n)! / ((n+1)! · n!)`: 1, 1, 2, 5, 14, 42, … Defined for `n ≥ 0`.

operators.name: Ceil
operators.description: Rounds a number up to the next largest integer

operators.name: CharacteristicPolynomial
operators.description: Characteristic polynomial det(x·I − A) of a square matrix (monic).

operators.name: ChineseRemainder
operators.description: Solve a system of simultaneous congruences: return the smallest non-negative integer `x` such that `x ≡ residues[i] (mod moduli[i])` for every `i`. Undefined if the system is inconsistent or the two lists differ in length.

operators.name: CholeskyDecomposition
operators.description: Cholesky decomposition of a positive-definite matrix.

operators.name: Choose
operators.description: Binomial coefficient: number of ways to choose k items from n. Agrees with Binomial for all defined values.

operators.name: Chop
operators.description: Replace tiny numeric values with zero.

operators.name: Chunk
operators.description: Split the collection into `k` nearly equal-sized chunks.

operators.name: CoefficientList
operators.description: Return the list of coefficients of a polynomial, from highest to lowest degree. Example: CoefficientList(x³ + 2x + 1, x) → [1, 0, 2, 1]

operators.name: Colon
operators.description: Type annotation (`a : b`) — opaque typed head.

operators.name: Color
operators.description: Parse a CSS-style color string to an Oklch color

operators.name: ColorContrast
operators.signature: (color | string | tuple, color | string | tuple) -> number
operators.description: APCA contrast ratio between two colors

operators.name: ColorDelta
operators.signature: (color | string | tuple, color | string | tuple) -> number
operators.description: Perceptual color difference (ΔE_OK) between two colors

operators.name: ColorFromColorspace
operators.description: Convert color space components to a canonical sRGB tuple

operators.name: Colormap
operators.description: Sample colors from a named palette

operators.name: ColorMix
operators.signature: (color | string | tuple, color | string | tuple, number?) -> color
operators.description: Mix two colors in OKLCh space

operators.name: ColorToColorspace
operators.description: Convert a color to components in a target color space

operators.name: ColorToString
operators.description: Convert a color to a string in the specified format

operators.name: Combinations
operators.description: Return all k-element combinations of a collection.

operators.name: Complement
operators.description: Return the elements of the first set that are not in any of the subsequent sets.

operators.name: Complex
operators.description: Construct a complex number from real and imaginary parts. Converted directly to a BoxedNumber during boxing; this entry exists so `operatorInfo("Complex")` returns a signature.

operators.name: ComplexRoots
operators.description: All n-th complex roots of a number.

operators.name: Condition
operators.description: Test whether a value satisfies one or more conditions.

operators.name: Congruent
operators.description: Indicate that two expressions are congruent modulo a number

operators.name: Conjugate
operators.description: Complex conjugate of a number.

operators.name: ConjugateTranspose
operators.description: Conjugate transpose (Hermitian adjoint) of a matrix or tensor.

operators.name: Contains
operators.description: Return True if the collection contains the given element, False otherwise.

operators.name: ContinuedFraction
operators.description: Return the continued-fraction expansion of `x` as a list of integer terms `[a0, a1, …]`. An exact rational is expanded fully; for an inexact value the expansion is truncated to the optional `n` terms (default 20).

operators.name: ContrastingColor
operators.signature: (color | string | tuple, (color | string | tuple)?, (color | string | tuple)?) -> color
operators.description: Choose the foreground color with better APCA contrast against a background

operators.name: CosIntegral
operators.description: Cosine integral: γ + ln(x) + ∫₀ˣ (cos(t)−1)/t dt.

operators.name: Count
operators.description: Return the number of elements in the collection.

operators.name: CountIf
operators.description: Return the number of elements in the collection satisfying the predicate.

operators.name: Cross
operators.description: Cross product of two 3-vectors.

operators.name: Csc
operators.description: Cosecant

operators.name: Csch
operators.description: Hyperbolic cosecant

operators.name: Cycle
operators.description: Produce an infinite sequence by cycling through the elements of a finite collection.

operators.name: D
operators.description: Symbolic partial derivative with respect to one or more variables.

operators.name: Declare
operators.description: Declare a symbol and optionally assign a type.

operators.name: DedekindEta
operators.description: Dedekind eta function η(τ), Im(τ) > 0.

operators.name: Degree
operators.description: Degree of an object

operators.name: Degrees
operators.description: Convert an angle in degrees.

operators.name: Delimiter
operators.description: Group expressions with explicit delimiters.

operators.name: Denominator
operators.description: Denominator of an expression

operators.name: Derivative
operators.description: Derivative operator that returns a derivative function.

operators.name: Determinant
operators.description: Determinant of a square matrix.

operators.name: Diagonal
operators.description: Extract a matrix diagonal or build a diagonal matrix.

operators.name: DictionaryFrom
operators.description: Create a dictionary from the elements of a collection of (key, value) pairs.

operators.name: Digamma
operators.description: Digamma function, the logarithmic derivative of the gamma function

operators.name: DigitCount
operators.description: Count digits of `n` in the given `base` (default 10); the sign of `n` is ignored. With a third argument `digit`, return how many times that digit occurs. Otherwise return a list `[count of 1, count of 2, …, count of base-1, count of 0]`.

operators.name: DigitsFrom
operators.description: Return an integer representation of the string `s` in base `base`.

operators.name: DigitSum
operators.description: Return the sum of the digits of `n` in the given `base` (default 10). The sign of `n` is ignored.

operators.name: Dimension
operators.description: Dimension of an object

operators.name: Discriminant
operators.description: Return the discriminant of a polynomial. Example: Discriminant(x² - 5x + 6, x) → 1

operators.name: Distance
operators.description: Euclidean distance between two points (tuples of numbers).

operators.name: Distribute
operators.description: Distribute multiplication over addition

operators.name: Divide
operators.description: Quotient of a numerator and one or more denominators.

operators.name: Divisors
operators.description: Return the sorted list of positive divisors of an integer `n`. The sign of `n` is ignored.

operators.name: DivisorSigma
operators.description: The divisor function σ_k(n) = Σ_{d | n} dᵏ over the positive divisors of `n`. σ₀ counts divisors, σ₁ sums them. Defined for `n ≥ 1`.

operators.name: DMS
operators.description: Construct an angle from degrees, minutes, and seconds.

operators.name: Dot
operators.description: Dot product (vector inner product) or matrix product.

operators.name: Drop
operators.description: Return the collection without the first n elements.

operators.name: DSolve
operators.description: Symbolic differential equation solver.

operators.name: Eigen
operators.description: Eigenvalue-eigenvector decomposition of a square matrix.

operators.name: Eigenvalues
operators.description: Eigenvalues of a square matrix.

operators.name: Eigenvectors
operators.description: Eigenvectors of a square matrix.

operators.name: EisensteinE
operators.description: Normalized Eisenstein series Eₛ(τ) of even weight s ≥ 2, Im(τ) > 0.

operators.name: Element
operators.description:
Test whether a value is an element of a collection. Optional third argument is a boolean expression (condition) for filtered iteration in Sum/Product.

Element supports two modes of operation:
1. Set membership: Element(3, [List, 1, 2, 3]) checks if 3 is in the list
2. Type-style membership: Element(x, integer) checks if x has type integer

Type-style membership works with:
- Mathematical sets: Integers, RealNumbers, ComplexNumbers, etc.
- Type names: integer, real, number, finite_real, positive_integer, etc.
- Invalid type names remain unevaluated (e.g., Element(2, "Booleans"))

operators.name: EllipticE
operators.description: Elliptic integral of the second kind: complete E(m) with one argument, incomplete E(φ|m) with two (amplitude first, parameter convention m = k², as in Mathematica).

operators.name: EllipticF
operators.description: Incomplete elliptic integral of the first kind F(φ|m) (amplitude first, parameter convention m = k², as in Mathematica). F(π/2|m) = K(m).

operators.name: EllipticK
operators.description: Complete elliptic integral of the first kind K(m), parameter convention m = k².

operators.name: EllipticPi
operators.description: Elliptic integral of the third kind: complete Π(n|m) with two arguments, incomplete Π(n; φ|m) with three (characteristic first, amplitude second, parameter convention m = k², as in Mathematica).

operators.name: Erf
operators.description: Gauss error function

operators.name: Erfc
operators.description: Complementary error function: 1 - Erf(x)

operators.name: Erfi
operators.description: Imaginary error function: -i·Erf(i·x)

operators.name: ErfInv
operators.description: Inverse of the error function

operators.name: Error
operators.description: Represent an error expression.

operators.name: ErrorCode
operators.description: Structured error code with optional arguments.

operators.name: Eulerian
operators.description: Eulerian number A(n, m): number of permutations of {1..n} with exactly m ascents.

operators.name: Evaluate
operators.description: Evaluate an expression.

operators.name: EvaluateAt
operators.description: Evaluate a function at one point or between two bounds.

operators.name: Exp
operators.description: Natural exponential function: e^x.

operators.name: Exp2
operators.description: Base-2 exponential: 2^x

operators.name: Expand
operators.description: Expand out products and positive integer powers

operators.name: ExpandAll
operators.description: Recursively expand out products and positive integer powers

operators.name: ExpIntegralEi
operators.description: Exponential integral Ei(x) = PV ∫_{−∞}^x eᵗ/t dt.

operators.name: ExtendedGCD
operators.description: Return the extended GCD of `a` and `b` as a tuple `(g, x, y)` where `g = gcd(a, b)` is non-negative and `a·x + b·y = g` (Bézout coefficients).

operators.name: Factor
operators.description: Factor a polynomial expression into a product of irreducible factors. Supports perfect square trinomials, difference of squares, and quadratic factoring with rational roots. Example: Factor(x² + 5x + 6) → (x+2)(x+3), Factor(x² + 2x + 1) → (x+1)²

operators.name: Factorial
operators.description: Factorial function: the product of all positive integers less than or equal to n

operators.name: Factorial2
operators.description: Double Factorial Function

operators.name: FactorInteger
operators.description: Return the prime factorization of an integer `n` as a list of `[prime, exponent]` tuples, ordered by ascending prime. For a negative `n`, a leading `[-1, 1]` tuple carries the sign.

operators.name: Fibonacci
operators.description: Compute the nth Fibonacci number.

operators.name: Fill
operators.description: Produce a 2D list (matrix) by applying a function to each pair of row and column indexes.

operators.name: Filter
operators.description:
Return the elements of the collection for which the predicate function returns True.

Equivalent to `[x for x in xs if p(x)]`.

operators.name: Find
operators.description: Return the first element of the collection satisfying the predicate, or Nothing if none found.

operators.name: FixedPoint
operators.description: Iterate a function until a fixed point is reached.

operators.name: Flatten
operators.description: Flatten a tensor or collection into a list.

operators.name: Floor
operators.description: Rounds a number down to the nearest integer.

operators.name: Fract
operators.description: Fractional part of a number: x - floor(x)

operators.name: FresnelC
operators.description: Fresnel cosine integral.

operators.name: FresnelS
operators.description: Fresnel sine integral.

operators.name: FromContinuedFraction
operators.description: Reconstruct the (rational) value of a continued fraction given its list of integer terms `[a0, a1, …]`.

operators.name: FromDigits
operators.description: Reconstruct an integer from its list of digits (most-significant first) in the given `base` (default 10). The inverse of `IntegerDigits`. Digits outside `[0, base)` are combined positionally (Horner evaluation).

operators.name: Function
operators.description: A function literal

operators.name: Gamma
operators.description: Gamma function Γ(z); with two arguments, the upper incomplete gamma Γ(s, z) = ∫_z^∞ tˢ⁻¹ e⁻ᵗ dt.

operators.name: GammaLn
operators.description: Natural logarithm of the gamma function.

operators.name: GCD
operators.description: Greatest Common Divisor

operators.name: GeometricVector
operators.description: Geometric vector (directed segment between two points) — opaque typed head. Distinct from the column-vector `Vector` operator.

operators.name: GraphemeClusters
operators.description: A collection of grapheme clusters from a string.

operators.name: GroupBy
operators.description: Partition the collection into a dictionary of lists based on the key returned by the function.

operators.name: HadamardProduct
operators.description: Hadamard (element-wise) product of two vectors or matrices of the same shape.

operators.name: Haversine
operators.description: Haversine function.

operators.name: Head
operators.description: Return the head of an expression, the name of the operator

operators.name: Heaviside
operators.description: Heaviside step function.

operators.name: Histogram
operators.description: Compute a histogram of the values in a collection. Returns a list of (bin start, count) tuples.

operators.name: Hold
operators.description: Hold an expression, preventing it from being canonicalized or evaluated until `ReleaseHold` is applied to it

operators.name: Hom
operators.description: Hom-set of morphisms between objects

operators.name: HorizontalSpacing
operators.description: Horizontal spacing annotation.

operators.name: Hsl
operators.description: HSL color (hue degrees, saturation/lightness 0-1, optional alpha)

operators.name: Hsv
operators.description: HSV color (hue degrees, saturation/value 0-1, optional alpha)

operators.name: Hypergeometric1F1
operators.description: Kummer confluent hypergeometric function ₁F₁(a; b; z) = M(a, b, z).

operators.name: Hypergeometric2F1
operators.description: Gauss hypergeometric function ₂F₁(a, b; c; z).

operators.name: Hypot
operators.description: Hypotenuse length: sqrt(x^2 + y^2).

operators.name: Identity
operators.description: Return the argument unchanged

operators.name: IdentityMatrix
operators.description: n-by-n identity matrix.

operators.name: If
operators.description: Conditional branch: evaluate one of two expressions.

operators.name: Imaginary
operators.description: Imaginary part of a complex number.

operators.name: IndexOf
operators.description: Return the 1-based index of the first occurrence of value in collection, or 0 if not found.

operators.name: IndexWhere
operators.description: Return the 1-based index of the first element satisfying the predicate, or 0 if not found.

operators.name: Infimum
operators.description: Like Min, but defined for open sets

operators.name: IntegerDigits
operators.description: Return the digits of `n` in the given `base` (default 10), most-significant first. The sign of `n` is ignored. With a third argument `length`, the result is zero-padded on the left (or truncated to its least-significant digits) to that length.

operators.name: IntegerSqrt
operators.description: Return the integer square root of `n`, i.e. the largest integer `m` such that `m² ≤ n`. Undefined for negative `n`.

operators.name: IntegerString
operators.description: `IntegerString(n, base=10)`       return a string representation of the integer `n` in base `base`.

operators.name: Integrate
operators.description: Symbolic integral with optional bounds.

operators.name: InterquartileRange
operators.description: Interquartile range (Q3 - Q1) of a collection.

operators.name: Intersection
operators.description: Return the intersection of two or more sets.

operators.name: Interval
operators.description: A set of real numbers between two endpoints. The endpoints may or may not be included.

operators.name: Inverse
operators.description: Multiplicative inverse of a square matrix.

operators.name: InverseFunction
operators.description: Inverse of a function.

operators.name: InverseHaversine
operators.description: Inverse haversine function.

operators.name: InvisibleOperator
operators.description: Implicit operator used for juxtapositions such as function application or multiplication.

operators.name: IsAbundant
operators.description: True if n is an abundant number (sum of divisors > 2n).

operators.name: IsCenteredSquare
operators.description: True if n is a centered square number.

operators.name: IsCompatibleUnit
operators.description: Check if two units have the same dimension

operators.name: IsComposite
operators.description: `IsComposite(n)` returns `True` if `n` is not a prime number

operators.name: IsDiagonal
operators.description: Whether the matrix is diagonal (all off-diagonal entries are zero).

operators.name: IsEmpty
operators.description: Return True if the collection is empty, False otherwise.

operators.name: IsEven
operators.description: Even Number

operators.name: IsHappy
operators.description: True if n is a happy number, a number which eventually reaches 1 when the number is replaced by the sum of the square of each digit

operators.name: IsOctahedral
operators.description: True if n is an octahedral number.

operators.name: IsOdd
operators.description: `IsOdd(n)` returns `True` if `n` is an odd number

operators.name: IsPerfect
operators.description: Returns "True" if n is a perfect number, a positive integer which equals the sum of all its divisors.

operators.name: IsPerfectPower
operators.description: Return `"True"` if `n` is a perfect power `a^b` for integers `a` and `b ≥ 2` (a negative `n` requires an odd exponent). The smallest perfect power is 4.

operators.name: IsPrime
operators.description: `IsPrime(n)` returns `True` if `n` is a prime number

operators.name: IsSame
operators.description: Compare two expressions for structural equality

operators.name: IsSatisfiable
operators.description: Check satisfiability using brute-force enumeration. O(2^n) complexity, max 20 variables.

operators.name: IsSquare
operators.description: True if n is a perfect square.

operators.name: IsSquareFree
operators.description: Return `"True"` if `n` is square-free (not divisible by any perfect square > 1). The sign of `n` is ignored.

operators.name: IsSquareMatrix
operators.description: Whether the value is a square matrix.

operators.name: IsSymmetric
operators.description: Whether the matrix is symmetric (A equals its transpose).

operators.name: IsTautology
operators.description: Check if expression is a tautology using brute-force enumeration. O(2^n) complexity, max 20 variables.

operators.name: IsTriangular
operators.description: True if n is a triangular number.

operators.name: Iterate
operators.signature: ((index: integer, acc:any) -> any, initial: any?) -> list
operators.description: Produce an infinite sequence by repeatedly applying a function to the previous value, starting with an initial value.

operators.name: JacobiSymbol
operators.description: The Jacobi symbol (a/n) for an odd `n > 0`. Returns -1, 0, or 1. Undefined when `n` is even or non-positive.

operators.name: JacobiTheta
operators.description: Jacobi theta function θⱼ(z, τ), j ∈ {1,2,3,4}, nome q = e^{iπτ} (Fungrim convention).

operators.name: Join
operators.description: Join the elements of some collections into a flat collection.

operators.name: Julia
operators.description: Smooth escape-time value for a Julia set with parameter c. Returns 1 for points inside the set, values in [0,1) for escaping points.

operators.name: Kernel
operators.description: Kernel (null space) of a linear map

operators.name: KeyValuePair
operators.description: A key/value pair

operators.name: KroneckerDelta
operators.description: Return 1 if the arguments are equal, 0 otherwise. With a single argument n, this is δ_{n,0}: 1 if n = 0, 0 otherwise.

operators.name: Kurtosis
operators.description: Kurtosis of a collection of numbers.

operators.name: LambertW
operators.description: Lambert W function (product logarithm)

operators.name: Latex
operators.description: Serialize an expression to LaTeX

operators.name: LatexString
operators.description: Value preserving type conversion/tag indicating the string is a LaTeX string

operators.name: Lb
operators.description: Base-2 Logarithm

operators.name: LCM
operators.description: Least Common Multiple

operators.name: LegendreSymbol
operators.description: The Legendre symbol (a/p) for an odd prime `p`. Returns -1, 0, or 1. Undefined when `p` is not an odd prime.

operators.name: Length
operators.description: Number of elements in a collection. Returns undefined for non-collections and for infinite collections.

operators.name: Lg
operators.description: Base-10 Logarithm

operators.name: Limit
operators.description: Limit of a function

operators.name: Limits
operators.description: Limits of a function

operators.name: Linspace
operators.signature: (start: number, end: number?, count: number?) -> indexed_collection

operators.name: ListFrom
operators.description: Create a list from the elements of a collection.

operators.name: Ln
operators.description: Natural Logarithm

operators.name: Log
operators.description: Log(z, b = 10) = Logarithm of base b

operators.name: Log10
operators.description: Base-10 Logarithm

operators.name: Log2
operators.description: Base-2 Logarithm

operators.name: LogIntegral
operators.description: Logarithmic integral li(x) = PV ∫₀ˣ dt/ln t = Ei(ln x).

operators.name: Loop
operators.description: Evaluate a body expression in nested iteration over Element clauses. Later clauses see earlier bindings; independent clauses produce a Cartesian product.

operators.name: LucasL
operators.description: Return the nth Lucas number: `LucasL(0)` is 2, `LucasL(1)` is 1, and `LucasL(n) = LucasL(n-1) + LucasL(n-2)`. Negative indices follow `LucasL(-n) = (-1)^n · LucasL(n)`.

operators.name: LUDecomposition
operators.description: LU decomposition of a square matrix.

operators.name: Mandelbrot
operators.description: Smooth escape-time value for the Mandelbrot set. Returns 1 for points inside the set, values in [0,1) for escaping points.

operators.name: Map
operators.description:
Return the collection where each element has been transformed by the mapping function.

Equivalent to `[f(x) for x in xs]`.

operators.name: Matrix
operators.description: Matrix constructor and canonicalizer.

operators.name: MatrixMultiply
operators.description: Matrix and vector multiplication.

operators.name: MatrixPower
operators.description: Square matrix raised to an integer power (repeated matrix product).

operators.name: MatrixRank
operators.description: Rank of a matrix (number of linearly independent rows/columns).

operators.name: Max
operators.description: Maximum of two or more numbers

operators.name: Mean
operators.description: Arithmetic mean of a collection of numbers.

operators.name: Median
operators.description: Median of a collection of numbers.

operators.name: Min
operators.description: Minimum of two or more numbers

operators.name: MinimalCNF
operators.description: Convert to minimal CNF using Quine-McCluskey. Max 12 variables.

operators.name: MinimalDNF
operators.description: Convert to minimal DNF using Quine-McCluskey. Max 12 variables.

operators.name: Mod
operators.description: Modulo: the remainder of the floored division of x by y. The sign of the result follows the sign of the divisor y (floored-division convention, matching most CAS). For a truncated/round-to-nearest remainder, see `Remainder`.

operators.name: Mode
operators.description: Most frequently occurring value in a collection.

operators.name: MoebiusMu
operators.description: Return the Möbius function μ(n): 0 if `n` is divisible by a perfect square > 1, otherwise (-1) raised to the number of distinct prime factors. The sign of `n` is ignored.

operators.name: Most
operators.description:
Return the collection without the last element.

If the collection has only one element, return an empty collection.

operators.name: Multinomial
operators.description: Compute the multinomial coefficient for multiple integers.

operators.name: MultiplicativeOrder
operators.description: The multiplicative order of `a` modulo `n`: the smallest `k > 0` such that `a^k ≡ 1 (mod n)`. Undefined unless `a` and `n` are coprime.

operators.name: Multiply
operators.description: Product of two or more values.

operators.name: N
operators.description:
N(expr): numerically evaluate an expression

N(expr, precision): evaluate to `precision` significant digits

operators.name: Nand
operators.description: Not-and: negation of conjunction

operators.name: ND
operators.description: Numerical derivative evaluated at a point.

operators.name: NDSolve
operators.description: Numerical differential equation solver.

operators.name: Negate
operators.description: Additive Inverse

operators.name: NextPrime
operators.description: Return the smallest prime greater than `n`. With a second argument `k`, return the kth prime after `n` (`k < 0` returns the |k|th prime before `n`).

operators.name: NIntegrate
operators.description: Numerical approximation of a definite integral.

operators.name: NLimit
operators.description: Numerical approximation of the limit of a function

operators.name: Nor
operators.description: Not-or: negation of disjunction

operators.name: Norm
operators.description: Vector or matrix norm.

operators.name: NotElement
operators.description: Test whether a value is not an element of a collection.

operators.name: NotSubset
operators.description: Test whether the first collection is not a strict subset of the second.

operators.name: NotSuperset
operators.description: Test whether the first collection is not a strict superset of the second.

operators.name: NotSupersetEqual
operators.description: Test whether the first collection is not a superset (possibly equal) of the second.

operators.name: NPartition
operators.description: Number of integer partitions of n.

operators.name: NthPrime
operators.description: Return the nth prime number (1-based): `NthPrime(1)` is 2, `NthPrime(2)` is 3, …

operators.name: Numerator
operators.description: Numerator of an expression

operators.name: NumeratorDenominator
operators.description: Sequence of Numerator and Denominator of an expression

operators.name: Oklab
operators.description: OKLab color (L 0-1, a/b ~ -0.4..0.4, optional alpha)

operators.name: Oklch
operators.description: OKLCh color (L 0-1, C 0-~0.4, hue degrees, optional alpha)

operators.name: OnesMatrix
operators.description: Matrix filled with ones.

operators.name: Ordering
operators.description: Return the indexes that would sort the collection.

operators.name: Pair
operators.description: A tuple of two elements

operators.name: Parse
operators.description: Parse a LaTeX string and evaluate to a corresponding expression

operators.name: PartialFraction
operators.description: Decompose a rational expression into partial fractions. Example: PartialFraction(1/((x+1)(x+2)), x) → 1/(x+1) - 1/(x+2)

operators.name: Permutations
operators.description: Return all permutations of length k (default full length) of a collection.

operators.name: PlusMinus
operators.description: Plus or Minus

operators.name: PolyGamma
operators.description: Polygamma function, the n-th derivative of the digamma function

operators.name: Polynomial
operators.description: Construct a polynomial from a list of coefficients (highest to lowest degree) and a variable. Example: Polynomial([1, 0, 2, 1], x) → x³ + 2x + 1

operators.name: PolynomialDegree
operators.description: Return the degree of a polynomial with respect to a variable. Example: PolynomialDegree(x³ + 2x + 1, x) → 3

operators.name: PolynomialGCD
operators.signature: (a: value, b: value, variable: symbol) -> value
operators.description: Return the greatest common divisor of two polynomials. Example: PolynomialGCD(x² - 1, x - 1, x) → x - 1

operators.name: PolynomialQuotient
operators.signature: (dividend: value, divisor: value, variable: symbol) -> value
operators.description: Return the quotient of polynomial division of dividend by divisor. Example: PolynomialQuotient(x³ - 1, x - 1, x) → x² + x + 1

operators.name: PolynomialRemainder
operators.signature: (dividend: value, divisor: value, variable: symbol) -> value
operators.description: Return the remainder of polynomial division of dividend by divisor. Example: PolynomialRemainder(x³ + 2x + 1, x + 1, x) → -2

operators.name: PolynomialRoots
operators.description: Return the roots of a polynomial expression. Example: PolynomialRoots(x² - 5x + 6, x) → {2, 3}

operators.name: PopulationStandardDeviation
operators.description: Population Standard Deviation of a collection of numbers.

operators.name: PopulationVariance
operators.description: Population variance of a collection of numbers.

operators.name: Position
operators.description: Return a list of indexes of elements in the collection satisfying the predicate.

operators.name: Power
operators.description: Exponentiation: raise a base to a power.

operators.name: PowerMod
operators.description: Return `a^b mod m` (modular exponentiation). A negative `b` uses the modular inverse of `a`; the result is undefined when that inverse does not exist (i.e. when `a` and `m` are not coprime). The result is in the range [0, m).

operators.name: PowerSet
operators.description: Return the power set of a set (set of all subsets).

operators.name: PreDecrement
operators.description: Decrement a number by one.

operators.name: Predicate
operators.description: Apply a predicate to arguments, returning a boolean

operators.name: PreIncrement
operators.description: Increment a number by one.

operators.name: Prime
operators.description: Derivative or prime notation (`f'`, `f^{(n)}`) — opaque typed head until a derivative library handler runs.

operators.name: PrimeFactors
operators.description: Return the sorted list of distinct prime factors of an integer `n`. The sign of `n` is ignored; `PrimeFactors(1)` is the empty list.

operators.name: PrimeImplicants
operators.description: Find all prime implicants using Quine-McCluskey. Max 12 variables.

operators.name: PrimeImplicates
operators.description: Find all prime implicates using Quine-McCluskey. Max 12 variables.

operators.name: PrimeNu
operators.description: Return ω(n), the number of distinct prime factors of `n`. The sign of `n` is ignored; `PrimeNu(1)` is 0.

operators.name: PrimeNumber
operators.description: The nth prime number. `PrimeNumber` is an alias for `NthPrime`, which is the preferred name.

operators.name: PrimeOmega
operators.description: Return Ω(n), the number of prime factors of `n` counted with multiplicity. The sign of `n` is ignored; `PrimeOmega(1)` is 0.

operators.name: PrimePi
operators.description: Return π(n), the prime-counting function: the number of primes less than or equal to `n`.

operators.name: PrimitiveRoot
operators.description: The smallest primitive root modulo `n` (a generator of the multiplicative group of integers mod `n`), or undefined if none exists (which happens unless `n` is 1, 2, 4, pᵏ, or 2pᵏ for an odd prime p).

operators.name: Product
operators.description: `Product(f, a, b)` computes the product of `f` from `a` to `b`

operators.name: PseudoInverse
operators.description: Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse of a matrix.

operators.name: QRDecomposition
operators.description: QR decomposition of a matrix.

operators.name: Quantity
operators.description: A value paired with a physical unit

operators.name: QuantityMagnitude
operators.description: Extract the numeric value from a quantity

operators.name: QuantityUnit
operators.description: Extract the unit from a quantity

operators.name: Quartiles
operators.description: Lower quartile, median, and upper quartile of a collection. Uses the Moore–McCabe (exclusive-hinges) convention: the sample is split at its median, and Q1/Q3 are the medians of the lower/upper halves with the overall median excluded from both halves when the sample size is odd.

operators.name: Radical
operators.description: Return the radical of `n` (its square-free kernel): the product of its distinct prime factors. The sign of `n` is ignored; `Radical(1)` is 1.

operators.name: Random
operators.description:
Random(): non-deterministic float in [0, 1)

Random(seed: real): deterministic float in [0, 1) from a real seed

Random(n: integer): non-deterministic integer in [0, n)

Random(m: integer, n: integer): non-deterministic integer in [m, n)

operators.name: RandomExpression
operators.description: Generate a random expression.

operators.name: RandomPrime
operators.description: Return a random prime. `RandomPrime(n)` draws a prime in [2, n]; `RandomPrime(m, n)` draws a prime in [m, n]. Undefined if the range contains no prime.

operators.name: Rank
operators.description: The length of the shape of the expression. Note this is not the matrix rank (the number of linearly independent rows or columns in the matrix)

operators.name: Rational
operators.description: Construct a rational number from a numerator and denominator.

operators.name: Real
operators.description: Real part of a complex number.

operators.name: RecordFrom
operators.description: Create a record from the elements of a collection of (key, value) pairs.

operators.name: ReleaseHold
operators.description: Release an expression held by `Hold`

operators.name: Remainder
operators.description: IEEE remainder: the signed remainder after dividing x by y, with the quotient rounded to the nearest integer (ties round toward +Infinity, matching JavaScript `Math.round`)

operators.name: Repeat
operators.description: Produce a sequence by repeating a single value. With 1 argument, returns an infinite sequence; with 2 arguments (value, count), returns a finite list of `count` copies.

operators.name: Reshape
operators.description: Reshape a tensor or collection to a target shape.

operators.name: Residue
operators.description: Residue of a function at a point (the coefficient of (x-a)⁻¹ in its Laurent expansion)

operators.name: Rest
operators.description:
Return the collection without the first element.

If the collection has only one element, return an empty collection.

operators.name: Resultant
operators.signature: (a: value, b: value, variable: symbol) -> value
operators.description: Return the resultant of two polynomials with respect to a variable. It is zero iff the polynomials share a common factor. Example: Resultant(x² - 1, x - 1, x) → 0

operators.name: Rgb
operators.description: sRGB color (channels 0-1, optional alpha 0-1)

operators.name: Root
operators.description: n-th root of a value.

operators.name: RotateLeft
operators.description: Rotate the elements of the collection to the left by n positions.

operators.name: RotateRight
operators.description: Rotate the elements of the collection to the right by n positions.

operators.name: Round
operators.description: Rounds a number to the nearest integer.

operators.name: RowReduce
operators.description: Reduced row echelon form (RREF) of a matrix.

operators.name: Rule
operators.signature: (match: expression, replace: expression, predicate: function?) -> expression
operators.description: Pattern replacement rule.

operators.name: Sample
operators.description: Return a random sample of k elements from the collection, without replacement. With an optional `seed` argument, the sample is deterministic.

operators.name: Sec
operators.description: Secant, inverse of cosine

operators.name: Sech
operators.description: Hyperbolic secant

operators.name: Segment
operators.description: Segment primitive — opaque typed head.

operators.name: Sequence
operators.description: Ordered sequence of expressions.

operators.name: SetFrom
operators.description: Create a set from the elements of a collection.

operators.name: SetMinus
operators.description: Return the set difference between the first set and subsequent values.

operators.name: Shape
operators.description: Return the shape tuple of an expression.

operators.name: Shuffle
operators.description: Randomize the order of the elements in the collection. With an optional `seed` argument, the shuffle is deterministic.

operators.name: Sigma0
operators.description: Number of positive divisors of n.

operators.name: Sigma1
operators.description: Sum of positive divisors of n.

operators.name: SigmaMinus1
operators.description: Sum of reciprocals of positive divisors of n.

operators.name: Sign
operators.description: Sign of a number: -1, 0, or 1.

operators.name: Signature
operators.description: Return the signature string of an operator.

operators.name: Simplify
operators.description: Simplify an expression.

operators.name: Sinc
operators.description: Unnormalized sinc function: sin(x)/x with sinc(0)=1.

operators.name: Single
operators.description: A tuple with a single element

operators.name: SinIntegral
operators.description: Sine integral: ∫₀ˣ sin(t)/t dt.

operators.name: Skewness
operators.description: Skewness of a collection of numbers.

operators.name: Slice
operators.signature: (value: indexed_collection, start: number, end: number) -> list
operators.description:
Return a range of elements from an indexed collection.

If the index is negative, it is counted from the end.

operators.name: SlidingWindow
operators.description: Return overlapping sliding windows of fixed size over the collection.

operators.name: Solve
operators.description:
Solve(equation, unknown): the list of solutions of an equation for the

unknown. The equation may be an `Equal` expression or a bare expression

(read as `= 0`), e.g. `Solve(x^2 - 1 == 0, x)` or `Solve(x^2 - 1, x)`.

operators.name: Sort
operators.description: Return the elements of the collection sorted according to the given comparison function.

operators.name: Sphere
operators.description: Sphere primitive — opaque typed head.

operators.name: Sqrt
operators.description: Square Root

operators.name: Square
operators.description: Square of a number: x^2.

operators.name: StandardDeviation
operators.description: Sample Standard Deviation of a collection of numbers.

operators.name: Stirling
operators.description: Stirling number of the second kind S(n, m): ways to partition n elements into m non-empty subsets.

operators.name: String
operators.description: A string created by joining its arguments. The arguments are converted to their default string representation.

operators.name: StringFrom
operators.description: Create a string by converting its arguments to a string and joining them.

operators.name: Subfactorial
operators.description: Compute the number of derangements (subfactorial) of n items.

operators.name: Subscript
operators.description: Subscript notation for indexing or compound symbols.

operators.name: Subset
operators.description: Test whether the first collection is a strict subset of the second.

operators.name: SubsetEqual
operators.description: Test whether the first collection is a subset (possibly equal) of the second.

operators.name: Subtract
operators.description: Difference between two or more values.

operators.name: Sum
operators.description: `Sum(f, [a, b])` computes the sum of `f` from `a` to `b`; `Sum(L)` sums the elements of a collection `L`

operators.name: Superset
operators.description: Test whether the first collection is a strict superset of the second.

operators.name: SupersetEqual
operators.description: Test whether the first collection is a superset (possibly equal) of the second.

operators.name: Supremum
operators.description: Like Max, but defined for open sets

operators.name: SVD
operators.description: Singular value decomposition of a matrix.

operators.name: Symbol
operators.description: Construct a new symbol with a name formed by concatenating the arguments

operators.name: SymmetricDifference
operators.description: Return the symmetric difference of two sets (elements in either set but not both).

operators.name: Tabulate
operators.description: Create a collection by applying a function to each index in the specified dimensions.

operators.name: Tail
operators.description: Return the tail of an expression, the operands of the expression

operators.name: Take
operators.description: Return `n` elements from a collection.

operators.name: Tally
operators.description: Return a tuple with the unique elements of the collection and their respective counts.

operators.name: Tanh
operators.description: Hyperbolic tangent

operators.name: Text
operators.description: A sequence of strings, annotated expressions and other Text expressions

operators.name: TildeEqual
operators.description: Approximately or asymptotically equal

operators.name: TildeFullEqual
operators.description: Indicate isomorphism, congruence and homotopic equivalence

operators.name: Timing
operators.description: `Timing(expr)` evaluates `expr` and return a `Pair` of the number of second elapsed for the evaluation, and the value of the evaluation

operators.name: To
operators.description: Action arrow / mapping (`a \to b`) — opaque typed head.

operators.name: Together
operators.description: Combine rational expressions into a single fraction

operators.name: Totient
operators.description: Euler's totient function φ(n): count of positive integers ≤ n that are coprime to n.

operators.name: Trace
operators.description: Trace of a matrix or pair of tensor axes.

operators.name: Transpose
operators.description: Transpose a matrix or swap two tensor axes.

operators.name: Triangle
operators.description: Triangle primitive — opaque typed head.

operators.name: Trigamma
operators.description: Trigamma function, the derivative of the digamma function

operators.name: Triple
operators.signature: (first: any, second: any, third: any) -> tuple<any, any, any>
operators.description: A tuple of three elements

operators.name: Truncate
operators.description: Rounds a number towards zero (removes the fractional part)

operators.name: TruthTable
operators.description: Generate truth table for expression. O(2^n) complexity, max 10 variables.

operators.name: Tuple
operators.description: A fixed number of heterogeneous elements

operators.name: TupleFrom
operators.description: Create a tuple from the elements of a collection.

operators.name: Type
operators.description: Return the type of an expression as a string.

operators.name: Unevaluated
operators.description: Prevent an expression from being evaluated

operators.name: UnicodeScalars
operators.description: A collection of Unicode scalars from a string, same as UTF-32

operators.name: Union
operators.description: Return the union of two or more collections as a set.

operators.name: Unique
operators.description: Return a list of the unique elements of the collection.

operators.name: UnitConvert
operators.description: Convert a quantity to a different compatible unit

operators.name: UnitDimension
operators.description: Return the dimension vector of a unit

operators.name: UnitSimplify
operators.description: Simplify a quantity unit to a named derived unit if possible

operators.name: Utf16
operators.description: A collection of UTF-16 code units from a string.

operators.name: Utf8
operators.description: A collection of UTF-8 code units from a string.

operators.name: Variance
operators.description: Sample variance of a collection of numbers.

operators.name: Vector
operators.description: Construct a column vector.

operators.name: When
operators.description:
Conditional/restriction value. `When(e, cond)` evaluates to:
  - `e` when `cond` evaluates to `True`
  - `Undefined` when `cond` evaluates to `False` (the "masking rule"; consumers like 2D plotters skip masked points)
  - `When(e, cond_simplified)` when `cond` is indeterminate (holds)
Stacked restrictions canonicalize: `When(When(e, c1), c2)` → `When(e, And(c1, c2))`.
Compiles to ternary `(cond) ? (e) : NaN` in JS and GLSL.

operators.name: Which
operators.description: Return the value for the first condition that is true.

operators.name: Wildcard
operators.description: Single-expression pattern wildcard.

operators.name: WildcardOptionalSequence
operators.description: Pattern wildcard matching zero or more expressions.

operators.name: WildcardSequence
operators.description: Pattern wildcard matching one or more expressions.

operators.name: Xor
operators.description: Exclusive or: true when an odd number of operands are true

operators.name: ZeroMatrix
operators.description: Matrix filled with zeros.

operators.name: Zeta
operators.description: Riemann zeta function

operators.name: Zip
operators.description: Combine multiple collections element-wise into a list of tuples. The result has the length of the shortest input.

constants.name: AvogadroConstant
constants.description: Avogadro constant

constants.name: BoltzmannConstant
constants.description: Boltzmann constant

constants.name: ComplexNumbers
constants.description: The set of all finite complex numbers.

constants.name: ContinuationPlaceholder
constants.description: This symbol indicates that some elements in a collection have been omitted, for example in a long list of numbers, or in an infinite set

constants.name: ElementaryCharge
constants.description: Elementary electric charge

constants.name: EmptySet
constants.description: The empty set, a set containing no elements.

constants.name: ExtendedComplexNumbers
constants.description: The set of all complex numbers, including infinities.

constants.name: ExtendedIntegers
constants.description: The set of all integers, including infinities.

constants.name: ExtendedRationalNumbers
constants.description: The set of all rational numbers, including infinities.

constants.name: ExtendedRealNumbers
constants.description: The set of all real numbers, including infinities.

constants.name: GasConstant
constants.description: Molar gas constant

constants.name: GravitationalConstant
constants.description: Newtonian constant of gravitation

constants.name: ImaginaryNumbers
constants.description: The set of all imaginary numbers.

constants.name: Integers
constants.description: The set of all finite integers.

constants.name: Mu0
constants.description: Vacuum permeability

constants.name: NegativeIntegers
constants.description: The set of all negative integers.

constants.name: NegativeNumbers
constants.description: The set of all negative real numbers.

constants.name: NonNegativeIntegers
constants.description: The set of all non-negative integers.

constants.name: NonNegativeNumbers
constants.description: The set of all non-negative real numbers.

constants.name: NonPositiveIntegers
constants.description: The set of all non-positive integers.

constants.name: NonPositiveNumbers
constants.description: The set of all non-positive real numbers.

constants.name: Numbers
constants.description: The set of all numbers.

constants.name: PlanckConstant
constants.description: Planck constant

constants.name: PositiveIntegers
constants.description: The set of all positive integers.

constants.name: PositiveNumbers
constants.description: The set of all positive real numbers.

constants.name: RationalNumbers
constants.description: The set of all finite rational numbers.

constants.name: RealNumbers
constants.description: The set of all finite real numbers.

constants.name: SpeedOfLight
constants.description: Speed of light in vacuum

constants.name: StandardGravity
constants.description: Standard acceleration due to gravity

constants.name: StefanBoltzmannConstant
constants.description: Stefan-Boltzmann constant

constants.name: VacuumPermittivity
constants.description: Vacuum permittivity (electric constant)
